# gomock

```go
import "go.uber.org/mock/gomock"
```

Package gomock is a mock framework for Go.

Standard usage:

```
(1) Define an interface that you wish to mock.
      type MyInterface interface {
        SomeMethod(x int64, y string)
      }
(2) Use mockgen to generate a mock from the interface.
(3) Use the mock in a test:
      func TestMyThing(t *testing.T) {
        mockCtrl := gomock.NewController(t)
        mockObj := something.NewMockMyInterface(mockCtrl)
        mockObj.EXPECT().SomeMethod(4, "blah")
        // pass mockObj to a real object and play with it.
      }
```

By default, expected calls are not enforced to run in any particular order. Call order dependency can be enforced by use of InOrder and/or Call.After. Call.After can create more varied call order dependencies, but InOrder is often more convenient.

The following examples create equivalent call order dependencies.

Example of using Call.After to chain expected call order:

```
firstCall := mockObj.EXPECT().SomeMethod(1, "first")
secondCall := mockObj.EXPECT().SomeMethod(2, "second").After(firstCall)
mockObj.EXPECT().SomeMethod(3, "third").After(secondCall)
```

Example of using InOrder to declare expected call order:

```
gomock.InOrder(
    mockObj.EXPECT().SomeMethod(1, "first"),
    mockObj.EXPECT().SomeMethod(2, "second"),
    mockObj.EXPECT().SomeMethod(3, "third"),
)
```

The standard TestReporter most users will pass to \`NewController\` is a \`\*testing.T\` from the context of the test. Note that this will use the standard \`t.Error\` and \`t.Fatal\` methods to report what happened in the test. In some cases this can leave your testing package in a weird state if global state is used since \`t.Fatal\` is like calling panic in the middle of a function. In these cases it is recommended that you pass in your own \`TestReporter\`.

<a name="InOrder"></a>
## func [InOrder](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/call.go#L438>)

```go
func InOrder(args ...any)
```

InOrder declares that the given calls should occur in order. It panics if the type of any of the arguments isn't \*Call or a generated mock with an embedded \*Call.

<a name="Call"></a>
## type [Call](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/call.go#L25-L45>)

Call represents an expected call to a mock.

```go
type Call struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Call.After"></a>
### func \(\*Call\) [After](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/call.go#L289>)

```go
func (c *Call) After(preReq *Call) *Call
```

After declares that the call may only match after preReq has been exhausted.

<a name="Call.AnyTimes"></a>
### func \(\*Call\) [AnyTimes](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/call.go#L85>)

```go
func (c *Call) AnyTimes() *Call
```

AnyTimes allows the expectation to be called 0 or more times

<a name="Call.Do"></a>
### func \(\*Call\) [Do](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/call.go#L155>)

```go
func (c *Call) Do(f any) *Call
```

Do declares the action to run when the call is matched. The function's return values are ignored to retain backward compatibility. To use the return values call DoAndReturn. It takes an any argument to support n\-arity functions. The anonymous function must match the function signature mocked method.

<a name="Call.DoAndReturn"></a>
### func \(\*Call\) [DoAndReturn](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/call.go#L114>)

```go
func (c *Call) DoAndReturn(f any) *Call
```

DoAndReturn declares the action to run when the call is matched. The return values from this function are returned by the mocked function. It takes an any argument to support n\-arity functions. The anonymous function must match the function signature mocked method.

<details><summary>Example (Capture Arguments)</summary>
<p>



```go
t := &testing.T{} // provided by test
ctrl := gomock.NewController(t)
mockIndex := NewMockFoo(ctrl)
var s string

mockIndex.EXPECT().Bar(gomock.AssignableToTypeOf(s)).DoAndReturn(
	func(arg string) any {
		s = arg
		return "I'm sleepy"
	},
)

r := mockIndex.Bar("foo")
fmt.Printf("%s %s", r, s)
// Output: I'm sleepy foo
```

#### Output

```
I'm sleepy foo
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Latency)</summary>
<p>



```go
t := &testing.T{} // provided by test
ctrl := gomock.NewController(t)
mockIndex := NewMockFoo(ctrl)

mockIndex.EXPECT().Bar(gomock.Any()).DoAndReturn(
	func(arg string) string {
		time.Sleep(1 * time.Millisecond)
		return "I'm sleepy"
	},
)

r := mockIndex.Bar("foo")
fmt.Println(r)
// Output: I'm sleepy
```

#### Output

```
I'm sleepy
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (With Overridable Expectations)</summary>
<p>



```go
t := &testing.T{} // provided by test
ctrl := gomock.NewController(t, gomock.WithOverridableExpectations())
mockIndex := NewMockFoo(ctrl)
var s string

mockIndex.EXPECT().Bar(gomock.AssignableToTypeOf(s)).DoAndReturn(
	func(arg string) any {
		s = arg
		return "I'm sleepy"
	},
)

r := mockIndex.Bar("foo")
fmt.Printf("%s %s", r, s)
// Output: I'm sleepy foo
```

#### Output

```
I'm sleepy foo
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Call.MaxTimes"></a>
### func \(\*Call\) [MaxTimes](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/call.go#L102>)

```go
func (c *Call) MaxTimes(n int) *Call
```

MaxTimes limits the number of calls to n times. If AnyTimes or MinTimes have not been called or if MinTimes was previously called with 1, MaxTimes also sets the minimum number of calls to 0.

<a name="Call.MinTimes"></a>
### func \(\*Call\) [MinTimes](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/call.go#L92>)

```go
func (c *Call) MinTimes(n int) *Call
```

MinTimes requires the call to occur at least n times. If AnyTimes or MaxTimes have not been called or if MaxTimes was previously called with 1, MinTimes also sets the maximum number of calls to infinity.

<a name="Call.Return"></a>
### func \(\*Call\) [Return](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/call.go#L188>)

```go
func (c *Call) Return(rets ...any) *Call
```

Return declares the values to be returned by the mocked function call.

<a name="Call.SetArg"></a>
### func \(\*Call\) [SetArg](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/call.go#L236>)

```go
func (c *Call) SetArg(n int, value any) *Call
```

SetArg declares an action that will set the nth argument's value, indirected through a pointer. Or, in the case of a slice and map, SetArg will copy value's elements/key\-value pairs into the nth argument.

<a name="Call.String"></a>
### func \(\*Call\) [String](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/call.go#L313>)

```go
func (c *Call) String() string
```



<a name="Call.Times"></a>
### func \(\*Call\) [Times](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/call.go#L228>)

```go
func (c *Call) Times(n int) *Call
```

Times declares the exact number of times a function call is expected to be executed.

<a name="Controller"></a>
## type [Controller](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/controller.go#L68-L78>)

A Controller represents the top\-level control of a mock ecosystem. It defines the scope and lifetime of mock objects, as well as their expectations. It is safe to call Controller's methods from multiple goroutines. Each test should create a new Controller.

```
func TestFoo(t *testing.T) {
  ctrl := gomock.NewController(t)
  // ..
}

func TestBar(t *testing.T) {
  t.Run("Sub-Test-1", st) {
    ctrl := gomock.NewController(st)
    // ..
  })
  t.Run("Sub-Test-2", st) {
    ctrl := gomock.NewController(st)
    // ..
  })
})
```

```go
type Controller struct {
    // T should only be called within a generated mock. It is not intended to
    // be used in user code and may be changed in future versions. T is the
    // TestReporter passed in when creating the Controller via NewController.
    // If the TestReporter does not implement a TestHelper it will be wrapped
    // with a nopTestHelper.
    T TestHelper
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewController"></a>
### func [NewController](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/controller.go#L84>)

```go
func NewController(t TestReporter, opts ...ControllerOption) *Controller
```

NewController returns a new Controller. It is the preferred way to create a Controller.

Passing [\\\*testing.T](<https://pkg.go.dev/testing/#T>) registers cleanup function to automatically call [Controller.Finish](<#Controller.Finish>) when the test and all its subtests complete.

<a name="WithContext"></a>
### func [WithContext](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/controller.go#L143>)

```go
func WithContext(ctx context.Context, t TestReporter) (*Controller, context.Context)
```

WithContext returns a new Controller and a Context, which is cancelled on any fatal failure.

<a name="Controller.Call"></a>
### func \(\*Controller\) [Call](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/controller.go#L195>)

```go
func (ctrl *Controller) Call(receiver any, method string, args ...any) []any
```

Call is called by a mock. It should not be called by user code.

<a name="Controller.Finish"></a>
### func \(\*Controller\) [Finish](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/controller.go#L247>)

```go
func (ctrl *Controller) Finish()
```

Finish checks to see if all the methods that were expected to be called were called. It is not idempotent and therefore can only be invoked once.

Note: If you pass a \*testing.T into [NewController](<#NewController>), you no longer need to call ctrl.Finish\(\) in your test methods.

<a name="Controller.RecordCall"></a>
### func \(\*Controller\) [RecordCall](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/controller.go#L168>)

```go
func (ctrl *Controller) RecordCall(receiver any, method string, args ...any) *Call
```

RecordCall is called by a mock. It should not be called by user code.

<a name="Controller.RecordCallWithMethodType"></a>
### func \(\*Controller\) [RecordCallWithMethodType](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/controller.go#L182>)

```go
func (ctrl *Controller) RecordCallWithMethodType(receiver any, method string, methodType reflect.Type, args ...any) *Call
```

RecordCallWithMethodType is called by a mock. It should not be called by user code.

<a name="Controller.Satisfied"></a>
### func \(\*Controller\) [Satisfied](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/controller.go#L256>)

```go
func (ctrl *Controller) Satisfied() bool
```

Satisfied returns whether all expected calls bound to this Controller have been satisfied. Calling Finish is then guaranteed to not fail due to missing calls.

<a name="ControllerOption"></a>
## type [ControllerOption](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/controller.go#L107-L109>)

ControllerOption configures how a Controller should behave.

```go
type ControllerOption interface {
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="GotFormatter"></a>
## type [GotFormatter](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L64-L68>)

GotFormatter is used to better print failure messages. If a matcher implements GotFormatter, it will use the result from Got when printing the failure message.

```go
type GotFormatter interface {
    // Got is invoked with the received value. The result is used when
    // printing the failure message.
    Got(got any) string
}
```

<a name="GotFormatterFunc"></a>
## type [GotFormatterFunc](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L73>)

GotFormatterFunc type is an adapter to allow the use of ordinary functions as a GotFormatter. If f is a function with the appropriate signature, GotFormatterFunc\(f\) is a GotFormatter that calls f.

```go
type GotFormatterFunc func(got any) string
```

<a name="GotFormatterFunc.Got"></a>
### func \(GotFormatterFunc\) [Got](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L76>)

```go
func (f GotFormatterFunc) Got(got any) string
```

Got implements GotFormatter.

<a name="Matcher"></a>
## type [Matcher](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L26-L32>)

A Matcher is a representation of a class of values. It is used to represent the valid or expected arguments to a mocked method.

```go
type Matcher interface {
    // Matches returns whether x is a match.
    Matches(x any) bool

    // String describes what the matcher matches.
    String() string
}
```

<a name="All"></a>
### func [All](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L335>)

```go
func All(ms ...Matcher) Matcher
```

All returns a composite Matcher that returns true if and only all of the matchers return true.

<a name="Any"></a>
### func [Any](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L338>)

```go
func Any() Matcher
```

Any returns a matcher that always matches.

<a name="AnyOf"></a>
### func [AnyOf](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L360>)

```go
func AnyOf(xs ...any) Matcher
```

AnyOf returns a composite Matcher that returns true if at least one of the matchers returns true.

Example usage:

```
AnyOf(1, 2, 3).Matches(2) // returns true
AnyOf(1, 2, 3).Matches(10) // returns false
AnyOf(Nil(), Len(2)).Matches(nil) // returns true
AnyOf(Nil(), Len(2)).Matches("hi") // returns true
AnyOf(Nil(), Len(2)).Matches("hello") // returns false
```

<a name="AssignableToTypeOf"></a>
### func [AssignableToTypeOf](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L432>)

```go
func AssignableToTypeOf(x any) Matcher
```

AssignableToTypeOf is a Matcher that matches if the parameter to the mock function is assignable to the type of the parameter to this function.

Example usage:

```
var s fmt.Stringer = &bytes.Buffer{}
AssignableToTypeOf(s).Matches(time.Second) // returns true
AssignableToTypeOf(s).Matches(99) // returns false

var ctx = reflect.TypeOf((*context.Context)(nil)).Elem()
AssignableToTypeOf(ctx).Matches(context.Background()) // returns true
```

<a name="Cond"></a>
### func [Cond](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L348>)

```go
func Cond[T any](fn func(x T) bool) Matcher
```

Cond returns a matcher that matches when the given function returns true after passing it the parameter to the mock function. This is particularly useful in case you want to match over a field of a custom struct, or dynamic logic.

Example usage:

```
Cond(func(x int){return x == 1}).Matches(1) // returns true
Cond(func(x int){return x == 2}).Matches(1) // returns false
```

<a name="Eq"></a>
### func [Eq](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L378>)

```go
func Eq(x any) Matcher
```

Eq returns a matcher that matches on equality.

Example usage:

```
Eq(5).Matches(5) // returns true
Eq(5).Matches(4) // returns false
```

<a name="GotFormatterAdapter"></a>
### func [GotFormatterAdapter](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L81>)

```go
func GotFormatterAdapter(s GotFormatter, m Matcher) Matcher
```

GotFormatterAdapter attaches a GotFormatter to a Matcher.

<a name="InAnyOrder"></a>
### func [InAnyOrder](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L445>)

```go
func InAnyOrder(x any) Matcher
```

InAnyOrder is a Matcher that returns true for collections of the same elements ignoring the order.

Example usage:

```
InAnyOrder([]int{1, 2, 3}).Matches([]int{1, 3, 2}) // returns true
InAnyOrder([]int{1, 2, 3}).Matches([]int{1, 2}) // returns false
```

<a name="Len"></a>
### func [Len](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L382>)

```go
func Len(i int) Matcher
```

Len returns a matcher that matches on length. This matcher returns false if is compared to a type that is not an array, chan, map, slice, or string.

<a name="Nil"></a>
### func [Nil](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L394>)

```go
func Nil() Matcher
```

Nil returns a matcher that matches if the received value is nil.

Example usage:

```
var x *bytes.Buffer
Nil().Matches(x) // returns true
x = &bytes.Buffer{}
Nil().Matches(x) // returns false
```

<a name="Not"></a>
### func [Not](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L402>)

```go
func Not(x any) Matcher
```

Not reverses the results of its given child matcher.

Example usage:

```
Not(Eq(5)).Matches(4) // returns true
Not(Eq(5)).Matches(5) // returns false
```

<a name="Regex"></a>
### func [Regex](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L417>)

```go
func Regex(regexStr string) Matcher
```

Regex checks whether parameter matches the associated regex.

Example usage:

```
Regex("[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}").Matches("23:02") // returns true
Regex("[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}").Matches([]byte{'2', '3', ':', '0', '2'}) // returns true
Regex("[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}").Matches("hello world") // returns false
Regex("[0-9]{2}").Matches(21) // returns false as it's not a valid type
```

<a name="WantFormatter"></a>
### func [WantFormatter](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L37>)

```go
func WantFormatter(s fmt.Stringer, m Matcher) Matcher
```

WantFormatter modifies the given Matcher's String\(\) method to the given Stringer. This allows for control on how the "Want" is formatted when printing .

<a name="StringerFunc"></a>
## type [StringerFunc](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L54>)

StringerFunc type is an adapter to allow the use of ordinary functions as a Stringer. If f is a function with the appropriate signature, StringerFunc\(f\) is a Stringer that calls f.

```go
type StringerFunc func() string
```

<a name="StringerFunc.String"></a>
### func \(StringerFunc\) [String](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/matchers.go#L57>)

```go
func (f StringerFunc) String() string
```

String implements fmt.Stringer.

<a name="TestHelper"></a>
## type [TestHelper](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/controller.go#L34-L37>)

TestHelper is a TestReporter that has the Helper method. It is satisfied by the standard library's \*testing.T.

```go
type TestHelper interface {
    TestReporter
    Helper()
}
```

<a name="TestReporter"></a>
## type [TestReporter](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/gomock/controller.go#L27-L30>)

A TestReporter is something that can be used to report test failures. It is satisfied by the standard library's \*testing.T.

```go
type TestReporter interface {
    Errorf(format string, args ...any)
    Fatalf(format string, args ...any)
}
```

## type [Barer](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/mockgen/internal/tests/alias/interfaces.go#L19-L21>)

Case 3: Generate mock for an interface that takes in alias parameters

```
and returns alias results.
```

type Barer interface {
    Bar(FooerAlias) FooerAlias
}
```

<a name="BarerAlias"></a>
## type [BarerAlias](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/mockgen/internal/tests/alias/interfaces.go#L24>)

```

<a name="Bazer"></a>
## type [Bazer](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/mockgen/internal/tests/alias/interfaces.go#L29-L31>)

Case 5: Generate mock for an interface that actually returns

```
the underlying type. This will generate mocks that use the alias,
but that should be fine since they should be interchangeable.
```

```go
type Bazer interface {
    Baz(Fooer) Fooer
}
```

<a name="Fooer"></a>
## type [Fooer](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/mockgen/internal/tests/alias/interfaces.go#L10-L12>)

Case 1: A interface that has alias references in this package

```
should still be generated for its underlying name, i.e., MockFooer,
even though we have the alias replacement logic.
```

```go
type Fooer interface {
    Foo()
}
```

<a name="FooerAlias"></a>
## type [FooerAlias](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/mockgen/internal/tests/alias/interfaces.go#L15>)

Case 2: Generating a mock for an alias type.

```go
type FooerAlias = Fooer
```

<a name="QuuxerConsumer"></a>
## type [QuuxerConsumer](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/mockgen/internal/tests/alias/interfaces.go#L45-L47>)

Case 7: Generate mock for a type that refers to an alias defined in another package

```
for an unexported type in that other package.
The generated method should only use the alias, not the unexported underlying name.
```

```go
type QuuxerConsumer interface {
    Consume(subpkg.Quuxer) subpkg.Quuxer
}
```

<a name="QuxerAlias"></a>
## type [QuxerAlias](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/mockgen/internal/tests/alias/interfaces.go#L36>)

Case 6: Generate mock for a type that refers to an alias defined in this package

```
for a type from another package.
The generated methods should use the alias defined here.
```

```go

```

<a name="Example"></a>
## type [Example](<https://github.com/uber/mock/blob/main/mockgen/internal/tests/generated_identifier_conflict/bugreport.go#L5-L16>)



```go
type Example interface {
    // _m and _mr were used by the buggy code: the '_' prefix was there hoping
    // that no one will use method argument names starting with '_' reducing
    // the chance of collision with generated identifiers.
    // m and mr are used by the bugfixed new code, the '_' prefix has been
    // removed because the new code generator changes the names of the
    // generated identifiers in case they would collide with identifiers
    // coming from argument names.
    Method(_m, _mr, m, mr int)

    VarargMethod(_s, _x, a, ret int, varargs ...int)
}
```
